textAPlaceholder: Paste or type text A here, or drop a file
textBPlaceholder: Paste or type text B here, or drop a file
tooLarge: File is too large to compare
manualHint: Large input — auto-compare disabled. Click Compare when ready.

descriptions.aeoDefinition: Text Diff is a free online tool for comparing two texts with line-level and word-level precision. Side-by-side or inline view with highlighted changes. Runs entirely in your browser — no data is sent to any server.
descriptions.whatIsP1: A diff is a structured comparison between two pieces of text that highlights what was added, what was removed, and what stayed the same. Developers use it every day to review code changes, audit configuration drift, and compare [JSON](/json) or [CSV](/csv) files.
descriptions.whatIsP2: This tool runs the comparison entirely in your browser using the jsdiff library. Nothing you paste or upload is sent to any server.
descriptions.useCasesP1: Comparing two configuration files ([JSON](/json), [YAML](/yaml), .env) to spot drift between environments.
descriptions.useCasesP2: Reviewing a code change before pasting it into a commit, especially when working outside an editor.
descriptions.useCasesP3: Reconciling two versions of a document, log, or query result side by side.
descriptions.step1Desc: Enter your original text in Text A and the modified version in Text B. You can also drag and drop files directly onto either input.
descriptions.step2Desc: Toggle between side-by-side (shows both texts with aligned changes) and inline (shows additions and deletions in a single stream).
descriptions.step3Desc: Added lines appear in green, removed lines in red. Word-level highlights within changed lines show exactly which words differ — the same convention used by GitHub pull requests.
descriptions.limitationsP1: The tool diffs text only. Binary files are detected and rejected to keep the result meaningful.
descriptions.limitationsP2: Inputs are capped at 5MB per side. Inputs above 512KB run in a background Web Worker; the page stays responsive but the diff itself takes proportionally longer for very large inputs.
descriptions.limitationsP3: Differences in line endings (CRLF vs LF) and trailing whitespace are normalized away — they will never appear as changes.
descriptions.faq1Q: Is there a file size limit?
descriptions.faq1A: Diff computation runs entirely in your browser using a Web Worker. Inputs up to 5MB per side are accepted; texts up to a few MB diff smoothly. Performance depends on your device.
descriptions.faq2Q: Can I ignore whitespace or case differences?
descriptions.faq2A: Yes. Use the Options menu to toggle 'Ignore whitespace' and 'Ignore case'. These settings filter out superficial changes so you can focus on meaningful differences.
descriptions.faq3Q: What algorithm does the diff use?
descriptions.faq3A: The tool uses the Myers diff algorithm via the jsdiff library. It first computes a line-level diff, then runs a second word-level pass on adjacent changed lines to highlight which specific words were added or removed.
